title: Fabric Interconnect Port-Channel Member Error Rates (Cisco UCS)
exclusions: Cisco Intersight–primary (or Intersight-only) Fabric Interconnect domains where the authoritative `cisco:ucs:stats` pull from classic UCSM is absent or not the data contract your operations runbook uses; FCoE-only or pure-FC Northbound port-channels in `fabric/san/…/fabricFcPc` and related SAN MO classes (treat with a storage-runbook, not this Ethernet LAN LAG use case); standalone server-facing access ports on IOMs that are not part of a `fabric/lan/…/pc-*` Northbound LAG; and aggregate LAG up/down and member-count state (covered by UC-19.1.5 as point-in-time channel health) — 19.1.13 is intentionally per-member error-rate trending, not a second copy of 19.1.5.
dataSources: `index=cisco_ucs` (or your org index) and sourcetype `cisco:ucs:stats` from the Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS. The statistics modular input polls UCS Manager for managed-object classes including `classId=etherErrStats` (per-port error counters on child rows of an `etherPIo` physical port), `classId=etherRxStats` (frame counters for a rate denominator), and `classId=fabricEthLanPcEp` (LAN LAG member endpoints) whose `epDn` references the `etherPIo` distinguished name, which lets you confine the math to true Northbound port-channel members rather than all fabric ports in the index.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731), v4.3.1 class of releases on Splunk Enterprise 9.1 through 10.2 and Splunk Cloud). The canonical app id on disk is `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` (underscore).
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-4h
| search (classId="etherErrStats" OR classId="etherRxStats")
| eval pio_key=replace(dn,"/[^/]+$","")
| where match(pio_key,"^sys/switch-")
| join type=inner max=0 pio_key [ search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-4h classId="fabricEthLanPcEp" | where match(dn,"^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-") | eval pio_key=coalesce(epDn,ep_dn) | eval parent_pc=replace(dn,"/ep-[^/]+$","") | table pio_key, parent_pc | dedup pio_key ]
| eval crc=if(classId="etherErrStats",coalesce(crcAlignErrs,crc_align_errs,0),null())
| eval fcs=if(classId="etherErrStats",coalesce(fcsErrs,fcs_errs,0),null())
| eval xmitD=if(classId="etherErrStats",coalesce(outDiscardEarly,out_discard_early,0),null())
| eval rcvE=if(classId="etherErrStats",coalesce(rcvErrs,rcv_errs,0),null())
| eval fr=if(classId="etherRxStats",coalesce(unicastRx,totalRx,unicast_rx,total_rx,0),null())
| sort 0 pio_key, classId, dn, _time
| streamstats current=f last(crc) as p_c last(fcs) as p_f last(xmitD) as p_xd last(rcvE) as p_re last(fr) as p_fr by dn
| eval crcAlignErrs_delta=if(classId="etherErrStats" AND isnotnull(crc),if(isnull(p_c),0,if(crc>=p_c,crc-p_c,crc)),null())
| eval fcsErrs_delta=if(classId="etherErrStats" AND isnotnull(fcs),if(isnull(p_f),0,if(fcs>=p_f,fcs-p_f,fcs)),null())
| eval xmitDiscard_delta=if(classId="etherErrStats" AND isnotnull(xmitD),if(isnull(p_xd),0,if(xmitD>=p_xd,xmitD-p_xd,xmitD)),null())
| eval rcvErrs_delta=if(classId="etherErrStats" AND isnotnull(rcvE),if(isnull(p_re),0,if(rcvE>=p_re,rcvE-p_re,rcvE)),null())
| eval total_frames_delta=if(classId="etherRxStats" AND isnotnull(fr),if(isnull(p_fr),0,if(fr>=p_fr,fr-p_fr,fr)),null())
| eval rowt=if(classId="etherErrStats","err",if(classId="etherRxStats","rx",null()))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(eval(if(rowt="err",crcAlignErrs_delta,0))) as crcAlignErrs_delta sum(eval(if(rowt="err",fcsErrs_delta,0))) as fcsErrs_delta sum(eval(if(rowt="err",xmitDiscard_delta,0))) as xmitDiscard_delta sum(eval(if(rowt="err",rcvErrs_delta,0))) as rcvErrs_delta sum(eval(if(rowt="rx",total_frames_delta,0))) as total_frames_delta by _time, pio_key, parent_pc
| rex field=pio_key "sys/switch-(?<fi>[AB])/slot-(?<slot>\d+)/(switch-)?(eth|aggr)-port-(?<port>\d+)"
| eval pkt_err=coalesce(crcAlignErrs_delta,0)+coalesce(fcsErrs_delta,0)
| eval error_rate_per_million=if(total_frames_delta>0,round(1000000*pkt_err/total_frames_delta,4),null())
| eval low_samp_bad=if(coalesce(crcAlignErrs_delta,0)>=1 AND (isnull(error_rate_per_million) OR total_frames_delta<1),1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(isnotnull(error_rate_per_million) AND error_rate_per_million>=1.0,"critical",isnotnull(error_rate_per_million) AND error_rate_per_million>=0.1,"warning",low_samp_bad=1,"investigate (short bin or low frames)",1=1,"ok")
| table _time, fi, slot, port, parent_pc, crcAlignErrs_delta, fcsErrs_delta, xmitDiscard_delta, rcvErrs_delta, total_frames_delta, error_rate_per_million, severity_bucket
| sort 0 - crcAlignErrs_delta
description: Trends per-member `etherPIo` error-counter deltas (CRC/alignment/FCS, transmit discard, and receive error counters) for Northbound `fabric/lan/…/pc-*` LAG members, normalised as errors per million good receive frames, using `classId=etherErrStats` and `classId=etherRxStats` in `cisco:ucs:stats` and an inner join to `fabricEthLanPcEp` so the results map back to a parent LAG. Complements aggregate `fabricEthLanPc` / `operState` health in UC-19.1.5 by surfacing pre-outage error ramps while the LAG can still be "up" at the channel level.
value: A Northbound LAG that still reports a healthy high-level state can already be burning on a single member: marginal optics, patch-panel strain, or a flakey transceiver often show up as a rising error rate long before a member is suspended or a link finally drops, which is when multipathed storage, live migration, and many VM networks see correlated loss. **Rate-normalising** with a receive-frame delta denominator stops a 100G member from being treated like a 10G one on raw counts alone, and the relationship to 19.1.5 makes the hand-off from "is the bundle logically healthy?" to "which leg is getting bit errors?" a deliberate workflow instead of a guess during a P1. The **streamstats** / reset handling also protects triage from UCSM `clear counters`, firmware waves, and counter-wrap edge cases that would make naive summation lie.
implementation: Enable the UCS statistics input so `cisco:ucs:stats` includes `etherErrStats`, `etherRxStats`, and `fabricEthLanPcEp`; run the `spl` with a 4–5 hour lookback in a test dashboard; verify `pio_key` maps to a Northbound LAG; then save as a 5m scheduled search with a throttle and optional lookup-backed thresholds, placing the panel next to UC-19.1.5 on the same FI operations dashboard.
visualization: (1) Heatmap of `fi` × `port` with cell colour from `error_rate_per_million`. (2) Line chart of `error_rate_per_million` over 24h for a shortlist of `parent_pc` (limit series so the panel stays legible). (3) Table of top-N `pio_key` rows by 24h cumulative `crcAlignErrs_delta` for hardware triage. (4) Single-value tiles counting rows in `severity_bucket` in `warning` and `critical`. (5) Optional per-member small-multiples sparkline of `error_rate_per_million` in Dashboard Studio for repeat offenders only.

references.title: Cisco UCS Manager XML API — managed objects and classes (use etherPIo, etherErrStats, etherRxStats; Programmers' Guide family)
Cisco UCS Manager Network Management Guide (port-channels, uplink monitoring, interface counters context)
Splunk — streamstats command reference (cumulative counter delta pattern)

knownFalsePositives:
• (a) Just after a link flap or UCSM clear counters, cumulative gauges reset: the streamstats if(curr>=prev,…) leg treats apparent negatives as a baseline reset, but a short suppression window (one to two minutes) after a documented change still prevents duplicate tickets while LACP converges.
• (b) 40G/100G breakouts: per-aggr-port and per-eth-port stats can both exist; summing them naively in an ad-hoc report double-counts the same physical lane. Pick one counting plane in architecture governance before comparing sites.
• (c) etherPauseStats on FCoE or PFC paths: PAUSE is flow-control, not a CRC. Pointing a CRC-oriented red tile at pause frames will page on normal storage bursts.
• (d) SFP-25G optics with marginal receive power near −12 dBm can show a low, steady epmf that is still within an optical budget; use two to three time bins and vendor thresholds before an RMA.
• (e) UCSM software activation that reboots IOM 2304 can reset uplink-related counters for many members in a five-minute window; align Splunk with the change record before blaming hardware.
• (f) Cable swaps and LACP re-convergence during planned work can show transient pre-converge errors; require multiple bad bins and Nexus-side counter agreement before a cable RMA.
• (g) Upstream Nexus 9000 with system MTU 1500 when storage paths expect 9216 jumbo to the FI can produce frameLong symptoms on bursty traffic; fix MTU on the switch, not the Splunk threshold alone.
• (h) clear counters in UCSM CLI during maintenance makes Splunk see a fresh delta epoch; the streamstats reset leg handles the maths, but operators may misread the alert as a clean bill of health on the wire — confirm Nexus show counters the same hour.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This use case is a deliberate companion to **UC-19.1.5**: 19.1.5 tells you if the Northbound Ethernet LAG is up and whether **LACP** members are present; **19.1.13** tells you whether physical members in that LAG are quietly accruing bit-level errors at a rate that matters before a member finally drops. Start with a green 19.1.5 row, then drill here for slow cable or optic faults. The two uses are complementary at triage time, not duplicates: you can have a healthy 19.1.5 channel while a single member's error rate climbs.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** on a **Heavy Forwarder** and Search Heads; enable the Cisco UCS statistics modular input to https://UCSM-VIP with index cisco_ucs (or your org standard). The only statistics sourcetype you should see for this pattern in current TA builds is **cisco:ucs:stats**. If any internal wiki still says **cisco:ucs:fi_stats**, that label is not a shipped FI statistics name — treat that wiki as stale and update it so new analysts do not run empty searches.
• The statistics profile must include **classId=etherErrStats**, **classId=etherRxStats**, and **classId=fabricEthLanPcEp**. If etherErrStats is not collected, you cannot compute **CRC/FCS** growth; if etherRxStats is missing, you can still see deltas as raw counts but you lose the rate denominator. Confirm with | stats count by classId on a 1h window.
• RBAC and network: the service account the modular input uses must read the **sys/switch-…** tree and the fabric/lan/... LAN port-channels; TLS to UCSM must be trusted (install full chain in the same trust store splunkd uses). UCSM poll skew of one to two minutes versus **Nexus** CLI is normal.

Step 1 — Configure data collection and build the error-rate contract
(1) In Splunk Web → Settings → Data inputs, open the Cisco UCS statistics input, set host, port 443, index, and the enabled MO list so etherErrStats, etherRxStats, and fabricEthLanPcEp are included (wording of checkboxes changes between minor TA releases; the classId table is the proof).
(2) On disk, you can also verify $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf (or a **Deployment Server** app overlay) for the right stanza, then restart the forwarder only as required by the release notes.
(3) Discovery probes you must be able to show an auditor: index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId — expect etherErrStats, etherRxStats, fabricEthLanPcEp, and usually etherTxStats; LAN fabricEthLanPc may appear and is not strictly required for this join (the join uses endpoints only).
(4) Field spelling: sample three etherErrStats events and add any alternate spellings to **coalesce()** in the spl. The UCS API and add-on generally emit camelCase keys, but do not hard-code a blog post; sample your own _raw.
(5) Cumulative versus sum-of-window (wrong): these MOs publish **monotonic** counters. You must not do | stats sum(crcAlignErrs) on raw poll rows for trend; that adds the running total once per event. The **streamstats** form with if(curr >= prev, curr-prev, curr) converts a **cumulative** series to deltas and treats **UCSM clear counters**, link flap, and **FI reboot** as a reset (the branch that uses the new absolute as the first increment) so you are not subtracting across a discontinuity. Counter wrap on 32-bit paths is rare on modern builds but when it happens the same if() leg treats the jump as a reset; if you still see spikes, cross-check Nexus.
(6) Optional threshold governance — create **lookups/ucs_fi_port_error_thresholds.csv** in a small team-owned app, referenced from **transforms.conf**, so dashboard tokens can set warn/crit by fi_model and port_speed_gbps instead of hardcoding 0.1 / 1.0 in every panel. Minimum seed (≥6 rows; edit to match your SFP policy; UTF-8, no BOM):

fi_model,port_speed_gbps,warn_per_million,crit_per_million,notes
UCS-6248UP,10,0.15,0.8,10G SFP+ baseline; tighten in lossless **FCoE**-adjacent designs
UCS-6248UP,40,0.12,0.7,QSFP/QSFP+ breakouts: avoid double counting **aggr+member** paths
UCS-6332-16UP,10,0.1,0.6,Common 10/25G mixed uplinks
UCS-6332-16UP,25,0.1,0.5,25G SFP: marginal receive power can show low epmf for days
UCS-6454,25,0.1,0.5,High-port FI in many M5/M6 blocks
UCS-6536,100,0.08,0.4,100G: absolute error counts are tiny; always use rate
UCS-6324,10,0.2,1.0,Smaller FIs; micro-bursts noisier, widen delay before page
UCS-6332,40,0.12,0.6,40G/4x10: separate PC versus IOM access paths in governance

Step 2 — Create the base search, two companions, and the alert
(1) Primary panel / alert — copy the spl field from this use case. Walk-through: **pio_key** is derived by stripping the last MO name from each dn so sys/switch-…/…-port-n is the stable key the fabricEthLanPcEp **epDn** also uses. The join to fabricEthLanPcEp is inner on purpose: only Northbound LAN port-channel members matching **^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-** are in scope — the same anchor as 19.1.5, so you do not accidentally include IOM-facing access ports that are not your Nexus-ward LAG.
(2) streamstats runs per dn (each stats child MO is its own time series) so etherErrStats and etherRxStats for the same port are independent series with different dn. Deltas for CRC and frames are both needed; after streamstats, a **bin _time** plus stats … by _time, pio_key, parent_pc fuses the err and rx streams in the same **5m bucket** so the numerator and denominator align in time. Emerging practice on 10/25G optical is to treat a steady pattern of at least one CRC-class increment on a modestly utilised member within about ten minutes as worth a pair of human eyes, while the eval thresholds in the spl map 0.1 and 1.0 errors per million good frames to warning and critical when the denominator is trustworthy.
(3) Companion A — 24h top members by delta CRC (table). Use only etherErrStats, compute pio_key, **inner-join** to fabricEthLanPcEp as in the primary search, sort by dn and _time, streamstats last() on coalesce(crcAlignErrs,crc_align_errs) by dn, compute delta with the same if(curr>=prev,...) guard, then **timechart** or stats by hour. If you use timechart sum() by pio_key, cap series for large domains.
(4) Companion B — **error_rate_per_million** trend: run the primary search through error_rate_per_million, then add | timechart span=1h p95(error_rate_per_million) by parent_pc with a strict limit= in production so the chart stays readable.
(5) Alert: schedule a 5–10 minute cron with a window covering at least two polls, throttle on pio_key, and route critical to your NOC path; keep warning in chat unless your SLO says otherwise. Suppress for 5–15 minutes after a known UCSM activation that reboots IOMs — the UCS fleet can show synchronised counter resets (see false positives). Do not schedule on */1 with a 60s poll and expect stable deltas — that often aliases the API cadence badly.

Step 3 — Validate against **show interface** counters, not only Splunk
(1) Pick a noisy pio_key, map fi/slot/port to the Nexus interface in your cable matrix, and run show interface … counters errors (syntax per platform) on both sides. If Nexus is clean but UCSM is not, you may be looking at the wrong member or a VLAN-scoped view; if Nexus matches, open an **RMA** triage with your SFP owner.
(2) In UCSM, navigate to the port-channel that matches parent_pc, then to each member; compare UCSM-shown counters to Splunk binned deltas in the same 5–15 minute window (poll skew).
(3) If **total_frames_delta** is null in many bins while errors exist, the **unicastRx** / totalRx coalesce list does not match your build — resample etherRxStats and extend coalesce().
(4) For FCoE-adjacent **PFC** designs, do not add **etherPauseStats** to this error runbook; **PAUSE** is back-pressure, not a CRC (see false positives). Track PAUSE in a separate storage SLO if the org needs it.
(5) If you migrated toward Cisco Intersight-primary for some FIs, classic UCSM stats may be absent for those domains; this is an exclusion — do not declare “no errors” from an empty index; validate governance first.

Step 4 — Operationalise
(1) Dashboard layout (one suggested board): row 1 = 19.1.5 tiles; row 2 = 19.1.13 primary as a timechart or table of error_rate_per_million; row 3 = top-N from Companion A; row 4 = heatmap of fi × port; row 5 = optional sparkline (**Dashboard Studio** child search). Add a drill token from 19.1.5 parent_pc to a form input on this board.
(2) **RACI**: Nexus team owns upstream MTU and vPC; UCS team owns FI and IOM; this UC is a shared bridge when **severity_bucket** is critical — put both aliases in the **PagerDuty** service mapping.
(3) Change calendar: a Nexus ISSU can increase errors briefly; UCSM clear counters (false positive h) makes Splunk deltas look like a fresh spike even when optics are fine — always cross-check the Nexus side the same hour.
(4) Evidence: export CSV of the panel plus a Nexus screenshot; store with the **Sev1** so vendor support does not wait a week for reproduction.

Step 5 — Tuning and troubleshooting (12 bullets)
• Class missing in stats count: the input is too narrow, or a UCSM upgrade renamed a class; expand MOs in the add-on, wait two polls, re-check.
• join empty: epDn empty, uplink not under ^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-, or you are in IOM space; re-read the exclusions and the Nexus-facing design doc.
• ok in Splunk but Nexus shows errors — wrong interface or LUN-path VLAN scope; re-map pio_key to patch panel labels with your RFO template.
• Huge epmf with frames≈0 — a post-reset or low-sample bin; add where total_frames_delta>1000 in alerting only after you have seen a sane floor in your environment.
• 100G/40G breakout and aggr+member double paths — if your TAM says count only leaf or only aggr, add where match/where !match on pio_key in Companion searches; never sum two layers in one KPI without a governance sign-off.
• FCoE PFC pause — not a link error; remove PAUSE from the same alert that treats CRC; put FCoE-specific SLOs in storage’s runbook, not the LAG runbook.
• **IOM 2304** reboot after firmware — many uplink counters reset at once; use a short post-activation suppression window with the change id.
• Cable swap during a change with LACP still converging — spikes for 1–2 bins; do not RMA on one 5m bin alone; require 2–3 bins in Companion B.
• Nexus 9K **MTU 1500** but jumbo storage to FI at 9k — systematic frameLong/drops; fix Nexus ip mtu and QoS policy per Cisco LAN design, not a lower epmf threshold that hides a config bug (see false positive g).
• **SFP-25G** at -12dBm — very low epmf can be tolerable; RMA only after TAM-aligned optical budgets; use 2–3 smoothed bins for trend, not a one-shot.
• Clock skew between UCSM, forwarder, and indexer can misorder two API samples; for Sev1, compare to UCSM GUI clock in the footer as 19.1.5 also advises.
• Index role missing cisco_ucs: the search looks empty; fix RBAC before SPL surgery — no amount of regex will surface data the role cannot read.

Closing: keep 19.1.5 and 19.1.13 next to one another. Aggregate health without per-member error trending is how cables that are still up keep browning out until a P1; trending without 19.1.5 is how you chase optics on a bundle you did not know was already degraded at the LACP layer. Together they are triage, not duplication.

If polls are sparse, widen bin span in Companion B; never page on a single isolated bin without a Nexus confirm in production. If you port this to a **summary index**, keep the same **join** and **reset** semantics; do not pre-sum cumulative counters in the summary because you would re-introduce the same double-count failure mode the **streamstats** stage is there to prevent. When you document the board in your team wiki, paste the primary **spl** plus a dated screenshot of `| stats count by classId` from your own cluster as evidence that **etherErrStats** and **etherRxStats** are actually present before you ask the NOC to trust a red panel. Add a short note to your runbook that Nexus-side show counters remains the second witness for any hardware RMA; Splunk is the early signal, not the only contract.
grandmaExplanation: We count tiny data mistakes on each physical wire in the fat uplink bundles that feed your server racks, even when the whole bundle still looks fine on a simple health check, so the right people can reseat, replace, or re-route work before a slow fault in one leg turns into a full link drop and a big outage. That is a different job from only asking whether a bundle is up or not.
hardware: Cisco FIs: 6248UP, 6296UP, 6332, 6332-16UP, 6454, 6536. IOMs: 2204XP, 2208XP, 2304, 2408. IFMs (UCS X-Series): UCS X9108-IFM-25G, UCS X9108-IFM-100G.
